patch.value: Unknown-command did-you-mean misses executable aliases: 'pm shwo' gets no suggestion (show/view/comment not in candidate set)

Repro: 'pm lsit' -> 'Did you mean: list?' (good) but 'pm shwo pm-xxxx' -> no did-you-mean at all, plus an unhelpful alphabetical Examples list (activity/aggregate/append). shwo->show is OSA distance 1 (transposition), but the EXECUTABLE_COMMAND_ALIASES tokens (show/view/comment/note/learning, src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts ~line 319) are rewritten pre-parse and apparently not included in the unknown-command suggestion candidates (error-guidance.ts unknownCommandNextSteps path). Fix: include executable alias tokens in the suggestion candidate list (suggest 'show' or its canonical 'get'). Telemetry already showed 'pm show' is the most common unknown-command shape, so typos of it are likely too.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented in feat/agent-ergonomics-cmd-suggestions-importer-ux. Executable command aliases (show/view->get, comment->comments, note->notes, learning->learnings) are now part of the unknown-command did-you-mean candidate pool. Exported EXECUTABLE_COMMAND_ALIASES from bootstrap-args.ts (single source of truth) and removed the redundant COMMON_COMMAND_ALIASES map in commander-usage.ts. buildUnknownCommandGuidanceFromRuntime now fuzzy-scores each alias TOKEN against the unknown command and attributes the score to the alias's CANONICAL command path, so 'pm shwo'->'Did you mean: get?', 'pm comemnt'->'comments', 'pm viee'->'get'. Real-command typos (lsit->list) still rank above aliases; aliases whose canonical command is absent from the runtime are not suggested. Tests: tests/unit/cli/cli-main-errors.spec.ts (alias-typo scoring + merge branches). Docs: AGENT_GUIDE.md Safe Defaults.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Evidence: continued branch feat/agent-ergonomics-cmd-suggestions-importer-ux on 2026-06-19. Temp-dir smoke confirmed pm shwo suggests canonical get. Focused UX tests passed (5 files, 255 tests). Repo gates passed after follow-up: pnpm quality:static, pnpm typecheck, node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage = 100/100/100/100 (230 files, 4653 tests).
